---
title: "TNTP Data Memo"
author: "Analyst at TNTP"
date: "`r Sys.Date()`"
output:
  word_document:
    fig_caption: yes
    reference_docx: tntp-style-file.docx
    toc: no
---

<!-- Initializing knitr -->
```{r setup,echo=FALSE}
# Initialize knitr
library(knitr) 

# set knitr options
opts_chunk$set(dev = "png", fig.width = 9, fig.height = 5.5, dpi = 300) # Sam uses these sizes in PPT slides but it's not scientific and maybe not ideal for Word  
opts_chunk$set(echo = FALSE)
opts_chunk$set(size = "small", background = "white")
opts_chunk$set(fig.path = "../reports/figures/fig-")
opts_chunk$set(highlight = TRUE, comment = NA, tidy = FALSE)
opts_chunk$set(knitr.table.format = "html")

# function for creating sequential figure numbers
figureN = local({
  i = 0
  function(x) {
    i <<- i + 1
    paste('Figure ', i, '. ', x, sep = '')
  }
})

# function for creating sequential table numbers
tableN = local({
  i = 0
  function(x) {
    i <<- i + 1
    paste('Table ', i, '. ', x, sep = '')
  }
})

```

<!-- Load all non-knitr packages with pacman -->

```{r load_packages, include = FALSE}

if (!require("pacman")) install.packages("pacman"); library(pacman)
p_load(readr, readxl, dplyr, magrittr, ggplot2, stringr, devtools, janitor)
if (!require("tntpr")) install_git("https://tools.tntp.org/bitbucket/scm/ct/tntpr.git"); p_load(tntpr)
```

<!-- Import data file(s) -->

```{r import_data}

# Import cleaned data file(s)
# Note: Use a common subdirectory structure
# Uncomment next line and change path to cleaned data file(s)
# read_csv("data/clean/my-file.csv")

```

<!-- The next line is a horizontal rule -->
***


<!------------------------ Executive Summary ------------------------>
##### Page Break
# Executive Summary
<br/> <!--  The <br/> tag is a way to introduce vertical spacing -->

This report is really empty. Please give me some better text here.


<!-------------------------- Key Findings --------------------------->
##### Page Break
## Key Findings
<br/>

* Key finding 1

* Key finding 2

* Key finding 3

<!------------------------- Recommendations ------------------------->
##### Page Break
## Recommendations
<br/>

* Recommendation 1

* Recommendation 2

* Recommendation 3


##### Page Break
<!-- An alternative way of handling section headings -->
Section Heading
=======================

Text...

Subheading
--------------------------------------------------------

More explanatory text...
<br/>

<!-- Use inline R and the function tableN to create a table caption.-->
`r tableN("Distribution of cars by cylinder count")`

```{r print_results_1, include=TRUE, echo=FALSE}

mtcars %>%
  tabyl(cyl) %>%
  kable(col.names = c("Cylinders", "Count", "Percent"), 
        align     = c("l", "c", "r"), 
        digits    = 2)

```

<br/>

Here's a crosstab showing distribution of engine cylinder by number of carburetors:
<br/>

`r tableN("Crosstab of cars by cylinder count")`

```{r cross_tab, include=TRUE, echo=FALSE}

mtcars %>%
  crosstab(carb, cyl) %>%
  adorn_crosstab(denom = "row", digits = 1, rounding = "half up") %>%
  kable(col.names = c("Carburetors", "4 cylinders", "6 cylinders", "8 cylinders"),
        align     = "lrrr")

```

##### Page Break

Here's that same data, visualized:
<br/>

```{r print_results_2, include=TRUE, echo = FALSE, message = TRUE, fig.cap=figureN("Carburetors vs. Engine Size in Older Vehicles")}

ggplot(mtcars, aes(x = carb, y = cyl)) +
  geom_jitter(color = palette_tntp("medium_blue"), height = 0.8, width = 0.4) +
  geom_smooth(method = "lm", se = TRUE) +
  theme_tntp() +
  labs(x     = "Number of carburetors", 
       y     = "Engine cylinders", 
       title = "Carburetors vs. Engine Size in Older Vehicles")

```
